description: Field manifest for the fortytwo persona templates. The CLI (fortytwo init) prompts for each field, stores answers in the gitignored .fortytwo/identity.json, and renders templates/**/*.tmpl into the project's gitignored context/. Every {{placeholder}} used in a template has an entry here.

fields.prompt: What is your assistant's name? (e.g. the persona it answers to)
Your full name (how the assistant refers to you)
Your primary email address (used for owner facts; never published)
Your base location or mailing address (city/country is enough; never published)
Your IANA timezone (e.g. Europe/Rome, America/New_York)
Your locale code (e.g. en-US, it-IT)
Languages you write in, comma-separated (the assistant matches what you use)
Default language when the assistant initiates with no recent inbound message
Any register/formality note for your language(s) (e.g. a preferred formal vs. informal address form; leave blank if none)
Your one-line role (e.g. 'Technical founder; CTO of <company>')
How the assistant should position you (the honest framing, not a hype label)
How you present yourself publicly (title + focus areas)
Public URLs about you (site, bio, profiles), one per line
A short paragraph: who you are as a person and professional
Your natural territory: the lenses through which you see problems, one per line
Factual biography anchors (milestones, ventures, roles), one per line
Personal topics you can credibly talk about, one per line
Example of a good angle on a personal topic (kept personal, not forced into a thesis)
Example of a bad angle (forcing a personal interest into a professional moral)
Your short bio
Your personal-voice bio
Your formal / events bio
Your interests (always-loaded summary), one per line
Your operating values and what you care about, one per line
Domains where you have real depth, one per line
Hard boundaries / do-not lines for representing you, one per line
Your core thesis / through-line belief
Signature phrases that capture your approach (orientation, not to be recited), one per line
Your beliefs across your working domains (markdown is fine)
Anti-patterns you explicitly reject, one per line
Your mini-manifesto (a few lines in your own voice)
Your final positioning rule (one sentence that survives the hype)
Your tone in one short phrase (e.g. 'reflective, dry, anti-hype')
Your formality level (e.g. casual, neutral, formal)
Your verbosity preference (e.g. terse, balanced, expansive)
Tone traits as a detailed list, one per line
One line capturing the essence of your voice
The typical shape your pieces take, one step per line
A short worked example of that structure
A short example showing your sentence rhythm
Words/phrases in your natural register (use freely), one per line
Words to use only when earned, one per line
Off-voice words to avoid, one per line
Things to never do when writing for you, one per line
Your main publishing channel (e.g. LinkedIn, X, blog)
What a good post of yours must have, one per line
Your preferred post length
Calls-to-action you like (soft/implicit), one per line
Calls-to-action you avoid, one per line
Themes that are authentically yours (markdown grouped by area is fine)
Your visual style rules for decks/graphics/content, one per line
One line: what your aesthetic should communicate
A reusable orientation prompt for writing in your voice
Your pre-publish quality-test questions, one per line
Name of your primary venture / domain
The correct one-paragraph narrative for that venture/domain
The operating layers of the domain (markdown is fine)
A synthetic formula for the domain (e.g. 'Input -> Intelligence -> Action')
The distinction that protects the positioning from being misread
The stronger position to claim
How the domain treats its core work (operating philosophy)
